fragility_points: Missing live-standings context could fully explain the high price.
Independent-source triangulation was weaker than ideal because web search failed in this runtime.
Tournament formats with playoffs and elite fields can punish overconfident single-player pricing.
key_assumptions: The 0.9505 price is not simply reflecting late-tournament standings already showing Sindarov near-certain.
No major FIDE ruling or withdrawal has quietly removed key rivals from contention.
The official eight-player field remains the operative competitive set.
main_logical_chain: Official FIDE source confirms Sindarov is legitimately qualified and the event format/winner logic are clear.
That same source shows a deep field and long format, so baseline tournament uncertainty remains substantial.
Because the market is pricing 95.05%, extra verification is required.
Extra verification did not uncover public evidence in this run that makes the outcome nearly settled.
Therefore the risk-manager view is bullish on Sindarov but materially below market confidence.
main_thesis: Sindarov is a real contender, but the visible evidence does not justify the market's 95.05% near-certainty absent hidden live-state context.
quote_anchors: The primary resolution source will be official information from FIDE
source_quality_view: Authoritative source-of-truth on field and rules is strong, but independent contextual verification was weaker than ideal due to search-tool failure.
strongest_disconfirmers: The same FIDE page shows a full elite eight-player double round-robin, which is hard to square with 95% for one player absent hidden context.
This run did not verify standings or rival ineligibility that would rationalize near-certainty.
strongest_supports: FIDE officially lists Sindarov in the 2026 Candidates field and as 2025 World Cup winner.
Polymarket rules designate official FIDE information as primary resolution source.
timing_relevance: High; if the event is already underway or nearly finished, standings could materially change the interpretation of the 95.05% price.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether the market snapshot reflects late-event state not visible in fetched sources.
Whether cross-runner pricing is coherent or structurally distorted.
Whether any qualifiers have since withdrawn or become unable to win.
what_would_change_view: Official FIDE standings showing Sindarov with a commanding late lead, rival ineligibility, or strong independent odds near 95% would move me toward the market.
